solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int maxLengthBetweenEqualCharacters(String s) {
        int[] firstIndex = new int[26];
        int[] lastIndex = new int[26];
        boolean[] found = new boolean[26];
        
        for (int i = 0; i < s.length(); i++) {
            int index = s.charAt(i) - 'a';
            if (!found[index]) {
                firstIndex[index] = i;
                found[index] = true;
            }
            lastIndex[index] = i;
        }

        int maxLen = -1;
        for (int i = 0; i < 26; i++) {
            if (found[i]) {
                maxLen = Math.max(maxLen, lastIndex[i] - firstIndex[i] - 1);
            }
        }

        return maxLen;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution tracks the first and last occurrence of each character using two arrays. It then calculates the maximum distance between any two equal characters, excluding the characters themselves. This approach is efficient with linear time complexity and constant space.
